symptom: Selecting a licensed Openverse result submits a new remote image URL and the production patch fails closed.
rootCause: Client conversion failure fell back to result.url while update_image intentionally permits remote URLs only unchanged for reframing.
fix: Embed the bounded Openverse thumbnail and never submit a new remote URL fallback.

symptom: A consented production web-research run cannot start because no supported search provider is configured.
rootCause: Production Convex has no LINKUP, Brave, Serper, or Tavily API key.
fix: Configure one NodeSlide-owned provider key, then rerun snapshot, region, and element-to-region acceptance.

symptom: Five of nine configured model routes return no one-token assistant text in the production fleet probe.
rootCause: Provider or model failures returned errors or empty output; exact per-route receipt retained without response bodies.
fix: Triage each failed provider route without relabeling the completed audit as fleet health.

symptom: An unrelated fallback chart lets induced requested-chart removal report clean without repairing the requested chart.
rootCause: Critique checks only whether any chart primitive exists, not whether the exact requested series survived.
fix: Carry exact requested chart semantics through the fault and revision contract, then rerun the DEV-only camera journey.

symptom: An unrelated fallback chart lets induced requested-chart removal report clean without repairing the requested chart.
rootCause: Critique previously checked only whether any chart primitive existed.
fix: Exact requested chart labels and values now flow through the synthetic fault and revision contract; DEV camera reduced exact issues 1 to 0.

symptom: Selecting a licensed Openverse result submits a new remote image URL and the production patch fails closed.
rootCause: Client fallback violated the embedded-image contract and production CSP omitted the consented Openverse thumbnail endpoint.
fix: Bounded embedded raster path plus Openverse connect-src allowlist; production camera and editable PPTX passed.

symptom: The generated narrow formula box stacks KaTeX characters vertically and is not visually legible.
rootCause: A broad .ns-element-math span rule applies wrapping and font overrides to every nested KaTeX span, compounded by a narrow generated formula element.
fix: Scope fallback text styles away from KaTeX descendants and visually re-prove the generated narrow formula.

symptom: The generated narrow formula box stacks KaTeX characters vertically and is not visually legible.
rootCause: A broad .ns-element-math span rule applied fallback wrapping and font overrides to every nested KaTeX span.
fix: Scoped fallback styles to direct code children, preserved KaTeX fonts/no-wrap, and added inline-size-aware narrow math sizing.

symptom: A consented production web-research run could not start because no supported search provider was configured.
rootCause: Production Convex previously had no supported search-provider credential.
fix: Configured the authorized production Linkup credential, preserved snapshots through projection, retained citation provenance in deterministic fallback, and kept element-bound snapshot context in Evidence.

symptom: Selecting a licensed Openverse result submits a new remote image URL and the production patch fails closed.
rootCause: Fixed: the client embeds the bounded Openverse thumbnail and never submits a new remote URL fallback.
fix: Embedded licensed raster path is production-proved and regression-covered.

symptom: A consented production web-research run cannot start because no supported search provider is configured.
rootCause: Fixed: the production Convex deployment has a supported Linkup credential configured without exposing its value.
fix: Production consent, retained sources, proposal, acceptance, and element-bound snapshot are proved.

symptom: An unrelated fallback chart lets induced requested-chart removal report clean without repairing the requested chart.
rootCause: Fixed: exact requested chart semantics survive the fault and revision contract.
fix: The induced loss is repaired and the deterministic critique regression remains green.

symptom: Product CSS can override nested KaTeX spans and collapse narrow formulas.
rootCause: Fixed: product CSS no longer overrides nested KaTeX spans.
fix: Chromium geometry and accessibility receipt plus deterministic tests are green.

symptom: Four of eight offered model routes fail the bounded production fleet probe.
rootCause: Kimi now passes with explicit reasoning disable; GLM exhausts 64 tokens, while Fable and two Gemini routes return provider errors under the shared disable shape.
fix: Inspect sanitized upstream error classes and implement route-specific reasoning controls in a fresh bounded QA pass.

symptom: Geometry-clean multi-archetype decks can still repeat one editorial text skeleton and replace diagrams or evidence with prose.
rootCause: Acceptance counted archetype IDs instead of rendered pixels, valid provider slides silently inherited fallback artifacts by position, and the creation schema had no typed diagram primitive or deck-rhythm gate.
fix: K1 implemented structured editable diagrams, honest provider normalization, and visual-rhythm revision signals. K2-K5 remain open for StorySpec/artifact inventory, reference-bound plans, rendered candidate critique/repair, and exported-PPTX visual acceptance.

symptom: Four of eight offered model routes fail the bounded production fleet probe.
rootCause: Fixed: Fable and both Gemini routes require reasoning, while GLM requires a supported high effort and a larger bounded visible-output budget.
fix: Provider metadata, UI effort allowlists, and route-specific probe profiles now agree; the fresh production audit passed 8/8.

symptom: Geometry-clean multi-archetype decks can still repeat one editorial text skeleton and replace diagrams or evidence with prose.
rootCause: Fixed: server-owned StorySpec and design plans bind honest materials and four annotated references to every slide; three rendered geometry candidates are scored fail-closed and feed the bounded repair loop.
fix: K1-K5 completed with an editable diagram, material inventory, design plans, 21 candidate renders, seven-slide rhythm review, and individually inspected PPTX renders after one collision repair.

symptom: Gemini 3.5 Flash returns a generic fallback deck for a World Cup brief while the creation action and PPTX export report success.
rootCause: The route can fall back after an unusable structured model response without surfacing that semantic substitution as a failed creation.
fix: Expose fallback provenance in the creation result and fail or visibly warn when the generated deck is not grounded in the requested brief.

symptom: Generated Kimi and Claude decks contain internal text collisions even though the PPTX overflow checker passes.
rootCause: The export validation detects content outside the canvas but does not detect overlapping text boxes or dense body and bullet regions inside slide bounds.
fix: Add internal overlap and minimum-legibility checks to generation critique and PPTX validation, then repair before export.

symptom: The 72-cell production Deck Gym baseline produced no promotion-ready deck; 66/70 exports lacked five meaningful visuals and 12 duplicate-deck groups crossed models or directions.
rootCause: Natural-language direction suffixes do not control a typed visual grammar or artifact inventory, and degraded routes reuse the same generic fallback deck.
fix: Make route provenance fail closed, require a typed slide-level artifact plan, and enforce corpus-level direction and novelty deltas before promotion.

symptom: Fifty-three of 70 exported Deck Gym decks used a degraded route; Gemini was degraded on all 24 cells and degraded decks preserved only 7.9% of frozen claims on average.
rootCause: Requested-model failure is converted into a generic fallback that still reaches the editor and export success path.
fix: Return a typed degraded result and require explicit acceptance before export; exclude degraded output from ranking and promotion.

symptom: Seventeen live-model decks contained 93 estimated overflowing text boxes although all 70 exported PPTX files passed the existing render and overflow test.
rootCause: Canvas-bound validation does not estimate line capacity inside individual text boxes, and the generation repair loop has no layout-derived copy budget.
fix: Add layout-derived word and line budgets plus targeted slide compression and exported-geometry preflight before download.

symptom: Two technical-architecture evidence-editorial cells reached the editor but never emitted a PPTX download within 90 seconds.
rootCause: The shared deck shape stalls an uninstrumented export phase; provider independence points to schema normalization or PPTX assembly rather than generation routing.
fix: Instrument normalization, media conversion, PPTX assembly, validation, and download separately, then reproduce from the preserved receipts.

symptom: Three qualified free models appeared in the production picker but createDeckFromBrief rejected their IDs at the Convex action boundary.
fix: Added all free-candidate literals and a catalog-to-validator drift regression test; deployed to agile-stoat-411.
rootCause: The shared model catalog was extended without updating the separately maintained nodeslideAgentModelValidator literal union.

symptom: The production frontend picker offered new routes but sent actions to blissful-pig-998 instead of the agile-stoat-411 production backend.
fix: Updated Vercel Production VITE_CONVEX_URL and added VITE_CONVEX_SITE_URL, then redeployed with explicit build env.
evidence: Production bundle inspection before fix: blissful-pig-998.convex.cloud; after fix asset index-B5Qg6_tU.js includes agile-stoat-411; Vercel deployment dpl_GqsV57GoBeLfJr32gre6e3fBLBvw.
rootCause: A manual Vercel source deployment consumed a stale project-level VITE_CONVEX_URL; the GitHub workflow pins the correct URL but the Vercel environment did not.

symptom: A live zero-cost Gemma run with positive token flow was labeled deterministic fallback and attribution degraded.
fix: Treat explicit :free routes with positive token flow as provider attempts and require candidateDigest only for patch-bearing runs.
evidence: artifacts/deck-gym/deck-gym-free-router-v1/screenshots/gemma-4-26b-editor.png; 2424 input and 1215 output tokens at cost 0.
rootCause: hasProviderAttemptTelemetry required costMicroUsd greater than zero and isFallbackTrace required candidateDigest even for creation runs without a patch.

symptom: The first zero-cost cohort pilot generated valid decks but added unsupported claims, substituted wrong visual primitives, repeated boilerplate, and exported raw LaTeX.
fix: Add claim-bound provenance, primitive-type matching, boilerplate rejection, and rendered formula checks before promotion.
rootCause: The creation contract validates JSON shape and geometry but lacks a claim allowlist, requested-artifact type equivalence, filler rejection, and exported formula-render fidelity gates.

symptom: A live zero-cost Gemma run with positive token flow was labeled deterministic fallback and attribution degraded.
fix: Treat explicit :free routes with positive token flow as provider attempts and require candidateDigest only for patch-bearing runs.
evidence: Production deployment dpl_BNzcn1xKQ3VWH5yyTa82mWPxhooQ; artifacts/deck-gym/deck-gym-free-router-v1/screenshots/gemma-4-26b-trace-live.png; live trace shows 2424 input and 1215 output tokens at $0.0000 with openrouter attribution.
rootCause: hasProviderAttemptTelemetry required costMicroUsd greater than zero and isFallbackTrace required candidateDigest even for creation runs without a patch.

symptom: Invisible planning text could satisfy rendered claim coverage
rootCause: Critic consumed planned text rather than rendered primitives
fix: Visible text is derived from emitted primitives only

symptom: Long model annotations clipped in the lower-right footer of browser and PowerPoint renders
rootCause: Annotation used an undersized footer box
fix: Moved annotation into a dedicated band above the evidence footer

symptom: Interrupted full render left a stale aggregate that undercounted eligible candidates during repair
rootCause: Partial mode merged against receipts.json instead of authoritative run receipts
fix: Added only-failed resume mode and per-candidate aggregation

symptom: Two screenshot candidates violate the artifact contract
rootCause: Gemma omitted missing-evidence labeling and Kimi used a forbidden screenshot claim
fix: Retained as red receipts for human review and adapter guidance

symptom: An editable 172 by 64 formula rendered as vertically stacked characters in the generated formula box.
rootCause: The broad .ns-element-math span rule applied wrapping and font overrides to nested KaTeX spans inside a narrow generated element.
fix: Preserved from the retired B6 camera worktree; superseded by the scoped KaTeX CSS fix and production proof.

symptom: An editable 172 by 64 formula rendered as vertically stacked characters in the generated formula box.
rootCause: The broad .ns-element-math span rule applied wrapping and font overrides to nested KaTeX spans inside a narrow generated element.
fix: Scoped fallback styles away from KaTeX descendants and verified narrow formula geometry, accessibility, and tests.
